gaps.snippet: nodule spore;
gaps.reason: derived nodule path `spore` has segment(s) [spore] colliding with a Mycelium reserved word — emitting them verbatim would fail to parse (`nodule spore;` tokenizes the colliding word as a keyword, not an identifier); each colliding segment is escaped with the `_kw` suffix (now `spore_kw`) rather than dropped, so distinct source files whose only differing segment is a reserved word (e.g. `l1.fuse` vs `l1.nodule`) stay distinguishable instead of both collapsing onto the same nodule path (G2/VR-5)

gaps.snippet:
# [doc = " Build a [`Spore`] from a parsed manifest and the project root directory."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " [`SporeError::Publish`] when a publish input is missing/ambiguous (no germination surface for a phylum,"] # [doc = " no `.myc` sources, a dependency without a `hash`, or an `[spore].include` naming a non-exported nodule),"] # [doc = " or [`SporeError::Io`] on a read failure. No partial artifact is produced (G2)."] pub fn build_spore (manifest : & Manifest , project_dir : & Path) -> Result < Spore , SporeError > { let kind = manifest . project . kind ; let exports = manifest . surface . as_ref () . map (| s | s . exports . clone ()) . unwrap_or_default () ; let include = manifest . spore . as_ref () . map (| s | s . include . clone ()) . filter (| i | ! i . is_empty ()) . unwrap_or_else (| | vec ! ["surface" . to_owned ()]) ; let mut surface : Vec < String > = Vec :: new () ; for entry in & include { if entry == "surface" { surface . extend (exports . iter () . cloned ()) ; } else { if manifest . surface . is_some () && ! exports . contains (entry) { return Err (SporeError :: Publish (format ! ("[spore].include names `{entry}`, which is not in [surface].exports — refusing to \
                     guess the germination surface (G2)"))) ; } surface . push (entry . clone ()) ; } } surface . sort () ; surface . dedup () ; if kind == ProjectKind :: Phylum && surface . is_empty () { return Err (SporeError :: Publish ("a phylum must declare its public [surface].exports (or [spore].include) — there is nothing \
             to germinate; the surface is never guessed (G2)" . to_owned () ,)) ; } let mut sources = collect_sources (project_dir) ? ; sources . sort_by (| a , b | a . path . cmp (& b . path)) ; if sources . is_empty () { return Err (SporeError :: Publish (format ! ("no `.myc` sources under {} — nothing to package" , project_dir . display ()))) ; } let mut deps = Vec :: with_capacity (manifest . dependencies . len ()) ; for d in & manifest . dependencies { let hash = d . hash . as_ref () . ok_or_else (| | { SporeError :: Publish (format ! ("dependency `{}` has no `hash` — an unpinned dependency is not reproducible; pin it \
                 (`hash = \"blake3:…\"`, ADR-003/G2)" , d . name)) }) ? ; deps . push (ResolvedDep { name : d . name . clone () , phylum : d . phylum . clone () , hash : hash . as_str () . to_owned () , version : d . version . clone () , }) ; } deps . sort_by (| a , b | a . name . cmp (& b . name)) ; let id = content_address (kind , & surface , & sources , & deps) ; Ok (Spore { id , kind , surface , sources , deps , name : manifest . project . name . clone () , version : manifest . project . version . clone () , }) }
gaps.reason: field access on a non-`self` base — the transpiler tracks no local types, so the projection cannot be resolved to a constructor position (only `self.<field>` desugars to a `match`)

qualified/associated-function call `String :: from` did not resolve to a known-emitted associated fn — Mycelium calls are bare identifiers, and the only sound surface form for `Type::method(...)` is the mangled `Type__method` name the declaration side already emits for a receiver-less inherent-impl associated fn (DN-34 §8.13/8.14), referenced only when that exact declaration is PROVEN present (same-file, or a resolved M-1084 cross-nodule sibling). A primitive/std associated fn with no emitted decl (e.g. `i128::try_from`), an unresolved type, or a `self`-receiving method (excluded by construction — it stays reachable only from its own bare call site) all gap here rather than fabricate a call (G2/VR-5, DN-133)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Append one length-prefixed field to the canonical pre-image: `<byte-length>:<bytes>\\n`. The byte"] # [doc = " count removes all delimiter ambiguity — the field spans exactly `v.len()` bytes, so an embedded"] # [doc = " space or newline cannot forge a record/field boundary (netstring-style canonicalization). This is"] # [doc = " what makes [`content_address`]'s `v1` encoding **injective** where `v0` was not."] fn push_field (s : & mut String , v : & str) { s . push_str (& format ! ("{}:{}\n" , v . len () , v)) ; }
gaps.reason: mutating method body statement is neither a `let`, a supported `self.<field>`/`*<param>` re-assignment, nor (in tail position) the method's own return value — value-threading deliberately refuses any shape outside a flat re-assignment sequence rather than risk an unsound rebind (DN-125 §6.1, never-silent G2/VR-5)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Collect every `.myc` source under `dir` (recursively), content-addressed by raw-byte BLAKE3. Skips"] # [doc = " hidden entries, `target/`, and the temp files a formatter might leave — deterministic and reproducible."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Bounded + symlink-safe (DN-40 §3).** The walk **does not descend into symlinked directory"] # [doc = " entries** (a symlinked-directory cycle would otherwise be an unbounded/infinite walk — a build"] # [doc = " DoS), and it caps nesting at [`MAX_WALK_DEPTH`], returning an explicit [`SporeError::Publish`]"] # [doc = " (never-silent; G2) rather than overflowing the stack."] fn collect_sources (dir : & Path) -> Result < Vec < SourceFile > , SporeError > { let mut out = Vec :: new () ; walk (dir , dir , 0 , & mut out) ? ; Ok (out) }
